answer.recommended_action: Replace or service the TEC assembly: ev.tec_load_check shows TEC current at 91% of hard limit (2.27 A / 2.5 A) with losing_setpoint=true and laser diode 5 °C above its 26 °C setpoint. Restoring thermal regulation is the critical path. Secondary: inspect and clean the optical window to close hyp.window_contamination (still open, log_odds=-0.60), since a fouled window could be a residual contributor. Disregard the reboot as causal (ev.trigger_demoted) and treat detector_temp_C as unreliable until the stuck-channel fault is resolved (ev.stuck_detector_temp_C).

hypotheses.label: Window contamination — particulate or film on the optical window attenuating outbound and/or return signal, consistent with gradual ~8 % range loss over days
Laser aging / output power decline — progressive reduction in emitted peak power reducing SNR at range, consistent with slow monotonic degradation
Detector sensitivity drift — dark-current increase or responsivity shift reducing receiver SNR, plausible over a week without thermal excursion
TEC / thermal regulation failure — rising detector or laser operating temperature degrading both emitter power and detector sensitivity, may present as range loss correlated with ambient temperature

Calibration / signal-processing parameter drift — stale range-gate threshold or gain coefficient causing effective detection cutoff to shift, detectable via config_diff against last known-good baseline

evidence.summary: config_diff returned no changed parameters; all signal-processing and range-gate thresholds match the last known-good baseline. No calibration or gain coefficient drift detectable in stored configuration.

TEC current is 2.27 A — 91 % of the 2.5 A hard limit — while the laser diode is held 5 °C above its 26 °C setpoint (measured 31.0 °C), with losing_setpoint=true. The TEC is saturated and no longer able to regulate diode temperature, directly implicating TEC thermal-regulation failure as the mechanism causing progressive SNR/range degradation. The check summary explicitly notes this implicates the TEC, not intrinsic laser aging.
log.reboot: degradation onset predates it → coincident, not causal
detector_temp_C is stuck (≈zero variance) → unreliable channel

trace.rationale: seed the differential from the symptom

All five hypotheses sit at equal log_odds=0.00 and zero evidence has been collected. config_diff is the highest-leverage first step: it directly tests hyp.calibration_drift by comparing current range-gate threshold, detection-cutoff, and gain coefficients against the last known-good baseline. The result is strongly binary — (A) a diff is found: hyp.calibration_drift rises sharply while all four hardware/optical hypotheses (window, laser, detector, TEC) fall, because a parameter shift fully accounts for the ~8% range loss without requiring any physical degradation; (B) no diff found: hyp.calibration_drift is effectively eliminated, concentrating probability on the physical hypotheses and making the subsequent hardware checks (detector_health_check, tec_load_check, spatial_intensity_check) far more targeted. No hardware access is needed, making this the fastest zero-risk discriminator before committing to intrusive checks.

All four remaining hypotheses sit at equal log_odds (0.40). TEC degradation is the only 'hub' cause that simultaneously degrades both laser output (via junction temperature) and detector sensitivity (via dark-current increase), so it subsumes laser_aging and detector_drift as secondary effects. A tec_load_check will surface duty-cycle saturation, setpoint error, or thermal runaway indicators. Result nominal → cleanly eliminates hyp.tec_degradation and refocuses investigation onto the three independent-pathway hypotheses (window_contamination, laser_aging, detector_drift). Result abnormal → strongly raises hyp.tec_degradation and partially explains hyp.laser_aging and hyp.detector_drift as downstream thermal effects, shifting them from independent root causes to symptoms — a large log_odds move in opposite directions for tec_degradation versus the other two. Window_contamination has no thermal dependence and is unaffected either way, keeping it as a parallel track. No prior evidence addresses thermal regulation; config_diff (ev.config_diff_clean) only cleared the calibration branch and says nothing about hardware health.
order onset vs the salient recent event; sanity-check channels
conclude or abstain from the differential
